name: fword
summary: 6-byte (48-bit) operand size (synonym: "pword"), e.g. a far pointer (16-bit selector + 32-bit offset) or a GDTR/IDTR pseudo-descriptor.

name: tbyte
summary: 10-byte (80-bit) operand size (synonym: "tword"), e.g. an x87 extended-precision float.

name: dqword
summary: 16-byte (128-bit) operand size (synonym: "xword"), for SSE XMM registers.

name: qqword
summary: 32-byte (256-bit) operand size (synonym: "yword"), for AVX YMM registers.

name: dqqword
summary: 64-byte (512-bit) operand size (synonym: "zword"), for AVX-512 ZMM registers.

name: ptr
summary: Marks the expression in brackets as a memory operand rather than an address computation, e.g. "byte ptr [rsi]" (the "ptr" keyword itself is optional in fasm/fasmg).

name: near
summary: Selects a near (in-segment) jump/call/pointer, using the full offset rather than a segment:offset far pointer.

name: far
summary: Selects a far (inter-segment) jump/call/pointer, encoded as segment:offset.

name: short
summary: Hints that a jump target is within a signed 8-bit displacement, so the assembler can encode it as a short (2-byte) jump instead of a near jump.
